freedict.name: FreeDict
freedict.description: Open-source bilingual dictionaries in StarDict/dictd/SLOB formats. Largest open bilingual dictionary project.
freedict.usage: Download .stardict.tar.xz, extract, use with GoldenDict/KOReader or parse .ifo/.dict/.idx files programmatically.

tatoeba.name: Tatoeba
tatoeba.description: Collaborative sentence database. 300+ languages. Sentence pairs, not dictionary entries. Updated weekly.
tatoeba.usage: Links files contain sentence ID pairs. Combine with sentences.tsv to get actual text. Or use per-language sentence files directly.

wiktionary.name: Wiktionary (Wikimedia Dumps)
wiktionary.description: Full wiki dumps in XML/SQL. Very rich multilingual data but requires heavy parsing.
wiktionary.usage: Parse with wiktextract (Python) or custom XML parser. Consider using Kaikki pre-extracted data instead.

kaikki-wiktextract.name: Kaikki.org (Wiktextract)
kaikki-wiktextract.description: Pre-extracted Wiktionary data in structured JSONL. By Tatu Ylonen. 25+ language editions. Includes definitions, translations, pronunciations, etymology, inflections.
kaikki-wiktextract.usage: JSONL format - one JSON object per line per word sense. Fields include: word, pos, senses (with glosses, translations), sounds (IPA), forms (inflections). Best structured open dictionary data available.
